title: Xtra Smoky Croatian Poppy Seeds Dumplings
description: A comforting Croatian dish featuring ground poppy seeds, pickle, and smoky flavors, perfect for a cold winter day. This kosher-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, ground poppy seeds, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the pickle juice, beaten egg, and water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 10 minutes or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, heat the vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and add them to the pan with the oil.
Pan-fry the dumplings for 2 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
